name: Razorpay Merchant API â€” JWT Authentication
scenario: Razorpay's payment API currently has no authentication. Any request can access any merchant's payments. Secure it with JWT authentication so merchants can only see their own data.

core.description: SecurityConfig @Configuration: @EnableWebSecurity, @EnableMethodSecurity. SecurityFilterChain: STATELESS session, CSRF disabled, /actuator/health permitAll, /api/admin/** requires ADMIN, all others authenticated. JWT resource server configured.
Custom JwtAuthenticationConverter: merchant_id as principal name, 'roles' claim â†’ ROLE_{role} authorities.
PaymentController: GET /api/payments â€” @PreAuthorize('hasRole(MERCHANT)'), merchant_id from jwt claim (not request param). GET /api/admin/payments â€” @PreAuthorize('hasRole(ADMIN)').
JwtAuditFilter OncePerRequestFilter: MDC with merchant_id + path + method, audit log after response, MDC.clear() in finally.
@WebMvcTest (4 tests): MERCHANTâ†’GET /payments=200, no JWTâ†’401, VIEWERâ†’GET /payments=403, ADMINâ†’GET /admin/payments=200. All 4 pass.
README: explain 401 vs 403 difference and why merchant_id must come from JWT claim.

bonus.description: Custom JWT validator: validates 'audience' claim includes 'razorpay-api' and 'merchant_id' claim is present. JwtValidationException on missing claims.
GET /api/auth/me: returns sub, merchant_id, roles, expiresAt. Test: response contains merchantId claim.
MerchantRateLimitFilter: 100 req/min per merchant_id using ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicInteger>. Returns 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. @EnableMethodSecurity: REQUIRED annotation on @Configuration for @PreAuthorize to fire. Without it, @PreAuthorize is silently ignored and all requests pass through. CSRF disable: safe for stateless REST APIs â€” CSRF requires session-based authentication. jwt() MockMvc processor: .with(jwt().claim("key","val").authorities(new SimpleGrantedAuthority("ROLE_MERCHANT"))) â€” need to explicitly set authorities for role checks. 401 vs 403: 401=unauthenticated (missing/invalid JWT), 403=authenticated but forbidden (valid JWT, wrong role). merchant_id from JWT claim: jwt.getClaimAsString("merchant_id") is tamper-proof (signed); request params are forgeable.
